item.title: It's time for yet another unhinged XL Thursday CeleryDump for the good & bad people of Imgur
item.description:
greetings online friends
i'm way too claustrophobic for that
not mine
as is tradition
that's ain't legal
when you've got the beach at 4 but a rodeo at 8
indeed
the same person in shorts during a blizzard
it has copper so you know it's good
love dippin dots (and pulled pork/mac n cheese)
every time
mmm hmm
nah
yes
it's not hard
nope
holler at your boy
that's my set.. have a great night, gang!!!
item.dc:creator: CeleryMan7

item.title: Proud of my kid
item.description: We had a rough couple of years. She was determined to...not be here...by now and we got through it. She graduated HS and is starting college in the fall. For now she's working overnight at Taco Bell. Warms my heart to see her dishing out replies that are both factual and funny. Moments like this make me feel like I got something right, even if I more often feel like I got everything wrong.
item.dc:creator: telemonianajax

item.title: What is a Sovereign Citizen?
item.description:
I’ve seen a few posts on Imgur about sovereign citizens and it seems to me that most people write them off as kooks and crazy people but they are much more dangerous than that. We’ve all seen the funny videos online where they get schooled by law enforcement or judges, and yes, I admit to finding those humorous as well. But I think dismissing them out of hand is a risky practice because many sovereign citizens are dangerous. I am not proclaiming to be an expert on this subject but have done a fair amount of research and think you should know about it. My primary sources are the Southern Poverty Law Center (SPLC), University of North Carolina, and FBI documentation. If you read this and want to know more, I would suggest you start there.
A sovereign citizen is someone that believes the government has no power of authority over them. This view is based on deliberate misinterpretation of law, contracts, and treaties. People generally refer to this misinterpretation of law by sovereign citizens as pseudolaw. The sovereign citizen movement started in the United States in the late 60s by a group of far right tax protestors and the first official organization was formed in 1971 called Posse Comitatus. Membership in this organization grew, and many others sprang up across the country. Although every organization (frequently referred to as an assembly) has its own unique version of their beliefs, there are a few that are common. 1) The sheriff is the ultimate law enforcement authority, and therefore they are not subject to any law enforcement other than the sheriff. 2) No law is enforceable unless there is a victim. 3) When the United States government did away with the gold standard for backing currency in the 30s, it enslaved its citizenry to back our money with the potential lifetime earnings of the citizen. 4) At some point in the past (dates vary widely) the United States government was taken over by The Admiralty, and the country is now run on commercial maritime law. 5) Each person born in the United States is two people. The first is the flesh and blood human being. The second is the “Trust” that the Treasury Department creates in your name at birth to take advantage of your earning potential. This is frequently referred to as “The Strawman”. 6) If you can legally separate yourself from the Strawman, you are not liable for any debts or crimes committed by the Strawman. You then become a “Free Person on the Land”
Some of the common tactics employed by sovereign citizens are: 1) Forging of documents 2) Counterfeiting of currency 3) Using legal documents to harass government officials and enemies. This is sometimes called paper terrorism. 4) Forging financial instruments and contracts 5) Squatting 6) Tax evasion It's common for sovereign citizens to file liens against properties they don’t own. Sometimes these are filed in the millions or billions of dollars and used to harass public officials. This is a particularly insidious tactic, because many times these fraudulent liens are not discovered until the owner goes to sell the property. It can take a long time to unravel in court. Squatting is another problematic tactic. They are taught to look for elderly people that live out of state and have unoccupied property like a house. They will break in, make a few improvements, and then file paperwork that the property was abandoned and take possession of it. They specifically look for out of state owners so that they are less likely to be notified of paperwork changes to their property. Elderly because they are less likely to understand what’s going on. Even if they do, it can take years to settle in court.
I think it’s important to understand that most people that identify as sovereign citizens have fallen victim to a scam themselves. The leaders of these organizations tend to prey on people that have little education, tax debt, and petty criminal records. If someone is a position where they owe a lot of money in taxes, can’t get a license or tags for their vehicle, and have other problems hindering the lifestyle they want to lead, and someone comes along and tells them that none of those things matter, all they have to do is file a few pieces of paper and they are free from their past, it looks very appealing. Of course the leaders of these groups sell tickets to seminars, how to guides, books, prepared legal documents, fake IDs, license plates, and all manner of other items to help people become sovereign. The thing to remember is that none of these pseudolegal arguments work in court. Its all a fiction. The people selling it know it doesn’t work and never has. However, there is money to be made victimizing these marginalized members of society.
One of the things sovereigns are taught is that government officials will not back down unless you firmly assert your sovereignty repeatedly. They are taught to yell and scream, to cause a scene. Some of the more extreme assemblies teach sovereigns to immediately respond with violence when confronted by law enforcement. Because they have been taught to resist, and because they have bought into this belief system with religious like zeal, violence by sovereign citizens is on the rise. Police officers have been shot and killed. Bystanders have been injured. Several of the people that were arrested for the January 6th attacks tried to use sovereign citizen defenses at their trials. Sovereigns have targeted family members of public officials for harassment. Some of them use fake law enforcement credentials to try to act under the color of authority. The movement is dangerous.
While the initial sovereign citizen movement had its roots in far right racism and anti semitic beliefs, organizations have sprung up to encompass most ethnic back grounds and political ideology. Some of these groups specifically target minorities and first generation immigrants. Often these are what is known as affinity crimes/Scams, as the leaders of the organization will be from the same background. Covid 19 was a big part of the expansion of the sovereign citizen movement. Many people that didn’t agree with lockdown and mask mandates got swept in. Instances of violence from sovereigns have increased since COVID. My hope is that in posting this, everyone will be more aware of what the movement is, and how dangerous it can be. If we just label them as kooks, and write them off, it becomes easier to disregard a growing danger in our society. On the other hand, its important to remember that many people associated with the movement are victims themselves.
item.dc:creator: crwoosley

item.dc:creator: Sensiblyinteresting

item.title: Am I being so sensitive?
item.description: Every time I express what I feel people around me are like "Now you are making me feel bad about." Like I usually do the housework despite all the paperwork that I take home. I wake up at 9 am, make breakfast, do my work, go to work and then when I get home I do our laundry. Sometimes, I cook dinner too while checking work again. I cannot breathe anymore. I don't want to talk to my family these days too because they only tell me about money and their problems. I am so drain. That's why I have tried to start saying NO when people around me ask favor like making breakfast. It is just that their respond is "Your work isn't my problem." I am speechless because these people are the same people I help when they have so much work to do. I am not asking for anything back but it just hurts.
item.dc:creator: myfanwy16

item.title: Memes vol 320
item.description:
Me posting memes to Imgur dot com
Me with @chriscaprisun and our 18 dogs, 2 cats, and 2 possums
MIFFY MIFFY MIFFY RUN!
Wasn’t one of her passwords “I hate gabe Duncan!”
:(
Racetrack Higgins!!
He’s SO CUTE!! I love American shepherds I must have 3
HES A PEKINGESE!! I love them so much I must have 4
I love chihuahuas I must have one
She’s so tiny she could have a bed in a Kleenex box
So I’m having this new thing where my cramps will start 4 DAYS BWFORE SHE ACTUALLY ARRIVES?? UM EXCUSE ME THIS ISNT YOUR TIME YET STAY IN YOUR LANE
This made me cry so much I’m getting my period THIS IS THE CUTEST THING I UAVE EVER SEEN!! Look at him in the car seat!! ( ✿˃̣̣̥᷄.⌓˂̣̣̥᷅ )
And then I suffer for it- I had an entire pint of ice cream the other day and y’all- it was rough. I think I can get away with it because I’ve been told I “have the metabolism of god” (??? Because god is known for his fast metabolism??) but no I most certainly cannot
Well other than that Mrs. Lincoln, how was the production?
This is the Book of Mormon and also Percy Jackson
Just today I said “yeah I’m invincible” so I might die in the next few days (but not before I turn 20! Which is in basically a week!)
This also made me cry- “I hold her and she is everything” GOOD BYE THAY IS THE SWEETEST
Liza Jane tax
Froggy tax
Scruffy tax
Nessie tax

item.title: The biggest Imgur celebration
item.description:
Hi everyone, I'm MrPuckett This weekend is a HUGE milestone and celebration on Imgur -
IT'S THE FIVE YEAR ANNIVERSARY OF THEFREEPIZZADUDE! FIVE YEARS DUDE!
It's crazy to think of where I was 5 five years ago, and even who I was 5 years ago
In August of 2019 I was living in this small town in the middle of nowhere, I didn't know anyone within a thousand miles I didn't have MrsPuckett, Puckett Jr or Nanna, it was just me and my little buddy Mr Puckett
I was still white-knuckling sobriety, afraid to fail every single day. I still had nightmares every night of my homeless days and everything that happened. I was posting a ton of wholesome memes and the interactions with all of you were such a huge part of my continued recovery.
This is where it all started back when I was homeless. My friend Rebecca in Arizona would sometimes order pizza and send it to whatever park or shelter I was sleeping at. When I got back on my feet I knew I wanted to be the person that she was.
... I soon realized how easy it was to send food to Imgurians, but I didn't have much money A few days after this post I got a $300 bonus at work, created a new username and spent a day sending Pizza to Imgurians in need ...
... I did my best to pretend it wasn't me at first, mainly because I thought it would be a one-and-done project and I would never post again. I could've never imagined the hundreds of messages that would flood TheFreePizzaDude inbox, mainly from people who wanted to help.
Each post I thought would be the last but those messages just kept coming from people who wanted to help I eventually created a discord server for the Pizza Angels and from there everything took off
Imagine all the best people you've never met all in the same server for one reason - to help people who need it. They've been there for me through everything - moves, heartbreak, mental health issues and even the passing of Mr Puckett
For Imgur it's a celebration of 5 years and thousands of people involved in helping people who need it But for me it's also a celebration of everyone I've grown so close to though this project
I've met more friends through TheFreePizzaDude than I have anywhere else in my life Hell the entire reason I moved to Nashville in 2020 was to be close to 2 amazing Imgurians I met in the project
So I have a favor to ask I want this to be an absolutely HUGE post and there's a lot of moving parts to make that happen But something really easy you can do right now is -
Set an alarm on your phone for 12pm central time Saturday Stop by usersub newest and drop us an upvote and a comment and celebrate all the incredible people that have made this project happen.
13,938 meals since we started 856 grocery orders sent One afternoon to celebrate all the amazing people I hope to see you there!!! ... If you can't make the Saturday post but still want to help, you can always contribute to the Imgurian Meal Fund and Pizza Angels will send the food for you! https://www.paypal.me/TheFreePizzaDude https://ko-fi.com/thefreepizzadude ... 12 pm central Saturday let's make it happen!
item.dc:creator: MrPuckett

item.title: And they were on sale…

Steve Urkel for suspense
I did it. I got my very first pair of REAL DOC MARTENS!!!!
AND THEY ARE PURPLE!!!! But dark enough that they’ll match all my fun leggings! My Blundstones have been serving me well over the past 7ish years but you can tell they are having a sadness.

To add, Algeria would never send transgender people to the Olympics, it's illegal to be transgender there!! https://www.the42.ie/womens-olympic-boxing-paris-games-iba-ioc-2-6452878-Aug2024/
